RULING:
DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION TO STAY FOR FORUM NON-CONVENIENS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's motion to stay the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Texas is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) If that failure is not determinate by itself, the balance of private and public interest factors likely weighs in favor of a stay as plaintiffs made no argument that such balance weighs in their favor. However, forum non conveniens is an equitable doctrine and given the evidence presented regarding Mr. Rodriguez's health, it would be inequitable to stay the action due to the likelihood that Mr. Rodriguez would die before resolving an action in either state. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
